name: Consensus by CoinDesk
edition: Consensus Miami 2026 (primary in-window edition). Note: two Consensus editions fell inside the June 2025 - June 2026 window - Consensus Hong Kong 2026 (Feb 10-12, 2026, Hong Kong Convention and Exhibition Centre) and the larger Consensus Miami 2026 (May 5-7, 2026). Consensus Hong Kong 2025 (Feb 18-20, 2025) was just BEFORE the window. This record focuses on Consensus Miami 2026.
location: Miami Beach Convention Center, Miami Beach, Florida, USA
dates: May 5-7, 2026 (Consensus Miami 2026). Companion in-window edition: Consensus Hong Kong 2026, February 10-12, 2026.
attendance: Marketed/expected at 20,000+ attendees from 100+ countries (some recaps cite a 15,000-20,000 range), including representatives from 200+ Fortune 500 companies. Program scale: 6 stages, 3-4 summits, 6 content tracks, 200+ sessions, and 500+ speakers. Institutional Summit (May 5) was application-only; previous editions cited participants representing roughly $4 trillion+ in AUM, with one recap estimating ~35% of attendees from institutional capital. Exact independently audited headcount not published.
defi_themes: Stablecoins as core financial infrastructure - dedicated multi-day Stablecoin Track / 'Stablecoin University'; reserve management, yield-bearing stablecoin products, cross-border payments and remittances, regulatory uncertainty, competition with CBDCs

Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization - real estate, treasury bills, bonds, private equity, commodities; fractional ownership and near-instant 24/7 settlement; RWA market cited crossing ~$31B (ex-stablecoins); 'Internet Capital Markets' framing
Institutional DeFi / TradFi-DeFi convergence - JPMorgan (Kinexys), Morgan Stanley, DTCC, Fidelity, Mastercard, State Street as sponsors/speakers; old 'TradFi vs DeFi' framing largely absent

AI agents in DeFi / 'Agentic Commerce' - autonomous agents executing on-chain transactions, managing liquidity positions, automating trading; programmable spending controls; standards like x402; agentic economy sized at $3-5T by 2030 across panels
DeFi & trading market structure - a stage dedicated to 'AI, DeFi & Trading' covering algorithmic/high-frequency strategies, decentralized exchanges (DEXs), liquidity and security challenges
Prediction markets and equity tokenization as institutional allocation themes
DeFi/stablecoin regulation and policy - SEC, CFTC and White House engagement; stablecoin legislation status
Privacy and accountability on public blockchains - hybrid blockchain architecture and address-level monitoring (compliance-friendly DeFi)

notable_panels.title: The RWA Engine and Institutional Frontier
notable_panels.topic: Institutional real-world-asset tokenization: building the infrastructure to bring traditional assets on-chain
notable_panels.speakers: Dennis Bree (Head of Institutional, Morpho), Angus Fletcher (Global Head of Digital Asset Solutions, State Street), Duane Block (Managing Director, Alvarez & Marsal Crypto Advisory)

notable_panels.title: Stablecoin Track / Stablecoin University (multi-day program)
notable_panels.topic: Full arc of stablecoins: regulatory discussion, reserve management, yield-bearing products, cross-border payments/remittances, enterprise adoption, and competition with CBDCs
notable_panels.speakers: Included PayPal (May Zabaneh, VP & GM of Crypto), MoneyGram, Circle, and MoonPay (CoinDesk University workshops led by Circle and MoonPay); Tether and Visa representatives also cited

notable_panels.title: AI, DeFi & Trading stage sessions
notable_panels.topic: Algorithmic and high-frequency trading, decentralized exchanges (DEXs), liquidity provisioning, security, and the regulatory landscape for on-chain markets
notable_panels.speakers: Not individually attributed in available coverage

notable_panels.title: Agentic Commerce track / Agentic University
notable_panels.topic: Autonomous AI agents executing on-chain transactions, managing liquidity, and automating trading; standards such as x402 and programmable spending controls
notable_panels.speakers: Not individually attributed; keynote on x402 listed on agenda

notable_panels.title: Institutional Summit (May 5, application-only)
notable_panels.topic: How institutional investors allocate into digital assets - tokenized assets, prediction markets, equity tokenization; 350+ one-on-one TradFi/crypto meetings
notable_panels.speakers: Asset allocators, hedge fund managers and banking executives (e.g., Morgan Stanley's Amy Oldenburg)

notable_panels.title: Privacy and accountability on public blockchains
notable_panels.topic: Argued privacy and compliance can coexist via hybrid blockchain architecture and address-level monitoring
notable_panels.speakers: Panelists from Moody's Ratings and ChangeNOW (per recap coverage)

notable_speakers.name: Amy Oldenburg
notable_speakers.affiliation: Head of Digital Asset Strategy, Morgan Stanley

notable_speakers.name: Angus Fletcher
notable_speakers.affiliation: Global Head of Digital Asset Solutions, State Street

notable_speakers.name: Duane Block
notable_speakers.affiliation: Managing Director, Alvarez & Marsal Crypto Advisory

notable_speakers.name: Paul Atkins
notable_speakers.affiliation: Chairman, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)

notable_speakers.name: Michael Selig
notable_speakers.affiliation: Chairman, U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC)

key_takeaways: Consensus Miami 2026 (May 5-7, Miami Beach Convention Center) was framed around 'Internet Capital Markets' - moving beyond speculation toward the technical and regulatory frameworks to host trillions in real-world assets on-chain.

Wall Street arrived in force: JPMorgan (Kinexys), Morgan Stanley, Fidelity, DTCC, Mastercard, State Street and others sponsored/spoke, and the old 'TradFi vs DeFi' dichotomy was largely absent - the dominant DeFi story was institutional convergence.

Stablecoins were positioned as the bridge between TradFi and crypto, with a dedicated multi-day track covering reserves, yield-bearing products, cross-border payments and CBDC competition; recaps noted settlement volume now rivaling major card networks, but stablecoin legislation timing/specifics remained unresolved.

RWA tokenization was a headline DeFi theme - real estate, treasury bills, bonds and private equity moving on-chain for fractional ownership and 24/7 settlement; the RWA market (ex-stablecoins) was cited crossing ~$31B, with institutional panels (Morpho, State Street) detailing the infrastructure.

'Agentic Commerce' - autonomous AI agents transacting, managing liquidity and trading on-chain via standards like x402 - emerged as the breakout new theme, repeatedly tied to a projected $3-5T agentic economy by 2030.

Heavy regulatory presence (SEC Chair Paul Atkins, CFTC Chair Michael Selig, White House advisor Patrick Witt, Senator Ashley Moody) signaled a shift from oversight to infrastructure-building dialogue, though stablecoin/DeFi rule clarity was still seen as a work in progress.

Classic permissionless DeFi primitives (lending protocols, perps, restaking, on-chain treasuries, yield farming) were present mainly through an 'AI, DeFi & Trading' stage and institutional lending (e.g., Morpho), but the overall DeFi narrative skewed toward stablecoins, RWA, institutional integration and AI agents rather than retail yield.

Two Consensus editions fell inside the research window: the Asia-focused Consensus Hong Kong 2026 (Feb 10-12, 2026) and the flagship Consensus Miami 2026 (May 5-7, 2026) - confirming the event is in-window.
